_notes: The 2022 SEP's 'City's Roadmap' section (p.55-56) lists prioritized actions in four phases. Years 1-5 uses a single continuous 1-28 numbering, which is what the 2025 Progress Report cross-references by number (e.g. 'Corresponds to Prioritized Action 24'). Years 6-10, 11-15, and 16-20 each restart their own 1-N numbering in the source and are not individually cross-referenced by the 2025 report.

years1to5.text: Adopt City of Greensboro recommendations.

Execute performance contract for highest energy usage Tier 1 building audits to: A. Address deferred maintenance on buildings and building equipment. B. Implement energy conservation in buildings with highest energy use.

status2025.text: See Buildings Managed by Engineering & Inspections, Goal 1.

years1to5.text: Adopt and implement hybrid/EV purchase policy for fleet vehicles (specifically police).
Establish criteria to identify the most impacted and vulnerable communities in the City.
Conduct HUB and MWBE certification workshops.
Housing & Neighborhood Development to schedule and provide energy and water efficiency education.
Establish funding for water and electricity conservation projects to assist lower-wealth residents in reducing demand.
Implement water/energy efficiency projects for lower-wealth residents.

status2025.text: See Recommendation 3, Strategy 3.4 (referenced in report as Strategy 3.4, content aligns with Strategy 3.5 on prioritizing vulnerable communities).

years1to5.text: Audit all City buildings for water efficiency opportunities.
Audit and fix distribution leaks.
Perform a study of other cities' water pricing structures.
Audit water and wastewater equipment and implement efficiency measures suggested by the audit.
Approve and provide funding for one MW Ground-Solar project at T.Z. Osborne.
Evaluate feasibility of rooftop solar project at Central Library.
Install energy conservation measures and new roof for Central Library.

status2025.text: The Central Library's new roof was installed in 2025. OSR is identifying funding to execute the needed maintenance and corrective measures identified through the library's Level 1 building energy audit.

years1to5.text: Install rooftop solar array on Central Library.
Approve Green Revolving Fund, establish initial budget, and develop a process for managing the fund.
Include SEP projects in Capital Improvements Program (CIP).
Conduct rooftop study for solar compatibility and capacity to identify rooftop solar projects.
Conduct feasibility study to determine compatibility of capturing waste heat from bio-solid incineration.
Request Duke Energy streetlamp conversion schedule.

status2025.text: See Transportation & Transit Departments, Goal 2 [streetlamp LED conversion tracking].

years1to5.text: Research green fee to determine if it is an option for the City.
Evaluate existing parking decks to determine if they can structurally support solar.
Create energy and water efficiency educational materials and distribute them through City channels.

status2025.text: See Water Resources Department, Goal 2 [GSO WaterWise Portal, water/energy conservation kits].

years1to5.text: Arts and Culture Program to develop a contest for creating a product out of common City waste/byproducts.
Review land use ordinances and building permit policies for obstacles to community solar installation.
Evaluate appropriate carbon offsets to achieve final emission reduction goals.
Evaluate and report yearly progress towards SEP goals.

years6to10.text: Execute performance contract for Tier 1 building audits not completed in Years 1-5 to: A. Address deferred maintenance on buildings and building equipment. B. Implement energy conservation in buildings with highest energy use.
Adopt and implement EV purchases for fleet vehicles.
Install appropriate EV charging stations to meet demand for City fleet vehicles.
Solarize projects at T.Z. Osborne or another site (1 MW each).
Identify, approve, and budget for identified rooftop solar projects.
Perform cost/benefit analysis of in-line hydroelectric project.
Identify energy efficiency renovations for the depot [J. Douglas Galyon Depot] that align with its historic designation and complete renovations.
Perform energy audit on the coliseum and complete conservation measures from the audit.
Perform energy audit on Cultural Center and complete conservation measures from the audit.
Conduct feasibility study of solar installation on existing parking decks.
Get next round of SEP projects added to CIP.
Evaluate and report yearly progress towards SEP goals.

years11to15.text: Identify, approve, and budget for additional rooftop solar projects.
Continue to convert remaining gasoline/diesel equipment to electric.
Continue to install charging stations to meet demand for City fleet vehicles.
Evaluate and report yearly progress towards SEP goals.

years16to20.text: Identify, approve, and budget for additional rooftop solar projects.
Continue to convert remaining gasoline/diesel equipment to electric.
Continue to install charging stations to meet demand for City fleet.
Purchase Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) to meet remaining emissions reduction goal.
Evaluate appropriate carbon offsets to achieve final emission reduction goals.
Evaluate and report yearly progress towards SEP goals.

note2025: The 2025 Progress Report states: 'Items lacking significant progress during 2025 are not included' from the Years 1-5 list — meaning Actions 1, 3, 5, 6, 9-14, 16-20, 22, 23, 25-27 have no 2025 status update in the source, which itself is an accountability-relevant gap (silence, not necessarily no activity).
